  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What primary help do you want for this section? Choose one: (A) writing polish (sentence/paragraph clarity and concision), (B) conceptual reframing and narrative restructuring of the contribution, (C) checking logical consistency and aligning claims with the reported evidence, or (D) positioning novelty versus prior work.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section asserts a main technical contribution, so reframing the thesis and contribution claim may be needed for clarity.",
    "R1.2": "The reasoning from conjugacy conditions to safety and speedup magnitude should be checked for a coherent argument chain.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about applicability (17/23 models) and speedups (1.2x–18x, median 3.4x) must align with the evidence reported.",
    "R1.6": "Improving sentence-level clarity, register, and concision can strengthen how the contribution is communicated.",
    "RX.1": "Any assistance should transparently convey uncertainty and the conditions under which claims hold.",
    "RX.2": "We should avoid overstating generality beyond the benchmarked models and conjugacy cases.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain scoped to helping with this section without expanding into new experiments or unrelated content.",
    "RX.4": "Wording should not encourage unsafe practitioner behavior, especially regarding silent skipping of the transformation.",
    "RX.5": "Edits or guidance should preserve traceability between claims and the specific numbers and statements in this section."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What primary help do you want for this section? Choose one: (A) writing polish (sentence/paragraph clarity and concision), (B) conceptual reframing and narrative restructuring of the contribution, (C) checking logical consistency and aligning claims with the reported evidence, or (D) positioning novelty versus prior work.

rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a main technical contribution, so reframing the thesis and contribution claim may be needed for clarity.
rationale.R1.2: The reasoning from conjugacy conditions to safety and speedup magnitude should be checked for a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about applicability (17/23 models) and speedups (1.2x–18x, median 3.4x) must align with the evidence reported.
rationale.R1.6: Improving sentence-level clarity, register, and concision can strengthen how the contribution is communicated.
rationale.RX.1: Any assistance should transparently convey uncertainty and the conditions under which claims hold.
rationale.RX.2: We should avoid overstating generality beyond the benchmarked models and conjugacy cases.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain scoped to helping with this section without expanding into new experiments or unrelated content.
rationale.RX.4: Wording should not encourage unsafe practitioner behavior, especially regarding silent skipping of the transformation.
rationale.RX.5: Edits or guidance should preserve traceability between claims and the specific numbers and statements in this section.
